meta.title: Icon Veneration is STILL an Accretion
meta.author: Gavin Ortlund

questions.question: Which council does Ortlund treat as defining icon veneration with anathemas and as binding for Roman Catholic and Eastern Orthodox infallibility claims in this video?
options: The Second Council of Nicaea (787)
The First Council of Nicaea (325)
The Council of Constantinople (381)
The Council of Chalcedon (451)
questions.answer: The Second Council of Nicaea (787)

questions.question: According to Ortlund’s summary of how the bishops describe it, what does an anathema principally mean in this context?
options: A temporary suspension from communion until penance
Separation from God, outer darkness, and condemnation—not a minor censure
A polite request to revisit one’s opinion
A purely political excommunication with no spiritual weight
questions.answer: Separation from God, outer darkness, and condemnation—not a minor censure

questions.question: Which cluster of practices does Ortlund say Nicaea II ties to icon veneration (not merely generic respect for religious art)?
options: Private journaling about sacred themes and silent meditation only
Burning incense only during domestic family prayer
Kissing, bowing or prostration, and lighting candles before images under figural mediation
Displaying unlabeled landscape paintings in parish halls
questions.answer: Kissing, bowing or prostration, and lighting candles before images under figural mediation

questions.question: What is “figural mediation” as Ortlund explains the council’s use of the idea?
options: Teaching doctrine only through spoken sermons without any visual aids
Rejecting all material creation as irrelevant to worship
Using parables alone so listeners imagine scenes mentally
Honor paid to the material image passes through to the person depicted (the prototype)
questions.answer: Honor paid to the material image passes through to the person depicted (the prototype)

questions.question: Regarding Basil of Caesarea’s famous line about honor passing to the prototype, what point does Ortlund (citing Mike Humphrey) emphasize?
options: Basil’s immediate context concerns the Trinity and the Son as image, not cultic veneration of church paintings
Basil denied that the Son is the image of the Father
Basil was clearly legislating parish icon corners in every city
Basil was responding to Minucius Felix on signet rings
questions.answer: Basil’s immediate context concerns the Trinity and the Son as image, not cultic veneration of church paintings

questions.question: Richard Price is cited as saying what about the iconophile historical narrative?
options: Icon veneration is the clearest apostolic practice in the first century
The iconophile view of Christian thought and devotion is virtually a denial of history
All ante-Nicene fathers explicitly taught kissing icons
Nicaea II added nothing new about tradition compared with earlier ecumenical councils
questions.answer: The iconophile view of Christian thought and devotion is virtually a denial of history

questions.question: In logic, what does Ortlund mean by “equivocation”?
options: Using two different languages in one sermon
Agreeing with an opponent for the sake of peace
Using the same word or phrase in different senses so an argument appears sound but is not
Citing only one manuscript family without variants
questions.answer: Using the same word or phrase in different senses so an argument appears sound but is not

questions.question: Which non-theological example does Ortlund give of equivocation involving “headache”?
options: Migraines vs tension headaches in medical literature
Literal head pain caused by loud music in church
Aspirin branding vs generic drugs
Annoying coworkers are a headache; painkillers cure headaches; therefore painkillers cure annoying coworkers
questions.answer: Annoying coworkers are a headache; painkillers cure headaches; therefore painkillers cure annoying coworkers

questions.question: The five ante-Nicene figures whose texts are rebutted in this video are:
options: Ignatius, Clement of Alexandria, Origen, Tertullian, and Methodius
Justin Martyr, Irenaeus, Hippolytus, Cyprian, and Athanasius
John Chrysostom, Basil, Gregory Nazianzen, Gregory of Nyssa, and Maximus
Augustine, Jerome, Ambrose, Leo the Great, and Cyril of Alexandria
questions.answer: Ignatius, Clement of Alexandria, Origen, Tertullian, and Methodius

questions.question: Whose YouTube response (second video) is Ortlund primarily engaging, alongside Michael Garten?
options: Trent Horn
Seraphim Hamilton’s channel
Craig Truglia
Jimmy Akin
questions.answer: Seraphim Hamilton’s channel

questions.question: How does Ortlund read Ignatius’s language about Christ setting up a “standard” (with Isaiah echoes)?
options: As mandating multiple physical banners in every altar area
As proof that Ignatius owned a collection of Roman eagles for worship
As metaphorical prophetic/military imagery about summoning the nations—not church flags to be kissed
As unrelated to Isaiah and solely about naval navigation
questions.answer: As metaphorical prophetic/military imagery about summoning the nations—not church flags to be kissed

questions.question: What equivocation does Ortlund allege when “standards are venerated by being raised” is used to reach Nicaea II?
options: From the date of Easter to the filioque
From Roman law to Jewish dietary codes
From baptismal formulas to creedal subordinationism
From veneration by exaltation of a banner to veneration by bowing, kissing, and figural mediation as at Nicaea II
questions.answer: From veneration by exaltation of a banner to veneration by bowing, kissing, and figural mediation as at Nicaea II

questions.question: In Clement of Alexandria’s discussion Ortlund highlights, what is the immediate topic?
options: Rules for modest dress and permissible signet seals versus idolatrous or violent images on rings
Dogmatic definition of the two natures of Christ
Instructions for painting dome mosaics of the Theotokos
A liturgy for weekly icon processions
questions.answer: Rules for modest dress and permissible signet seals versus idolatrous or violent images on rings

questions.question: Why does Ortlund object to using Pliny the Elder on how signet rings were stored as evidence for Clement’s Christian icon theology?
options: Pliny was a fourth-century bishop at Nicaea II
Pliny was not a Christian author and Clement does not adopt Pliny’s “sanctuary” language for rings
Clement quotes Pliny verbatim as binding canon law
Pliny only wrote about Egyptian animal gods, not rings
questions.answer: Pliny was not a Christian author and Clement does not adopt Pliny’s “sanctuary” language for rings

questions.question: In Origen’s homily passage discussed, what are shallow Christians said to do alongside bowing to priests?
options: Reject the Old Testament entirely
Destroy every image in the empire
Bring something for the decoration of the altar or church while not reforming their lives
Compose new ecumenical canons on images
questions.answer: Bring something for the decoration of the altar or church while not reforming their lives

questions.question: What does Ortlund argue is the main problem with reading that Origen passage as proof of Nicaea II-style veneration?
options: Origen explicitly names every icon by artist and date
The homily is a later forgery from the tenth century
Origen requires candles only at home altars, never in church
Equivocation from adornment/decoration to bowing and kissing icons under figural mediation; no icons mentioned
questions.answer: Equivocation from adornment/decoration to bowing and kissing icons under figural mediation; no icons mentioned

questions.question: According to Ortlund’s appeal to Contra Celsum, how does Origen respond to the charge that Christians lack images?
options: He affirms Christians do not use images in worship and gives a distinct rationale from other image-less groups
He clarifies that only pagan images are wrong but Christian cult images are mandatory
He says Christians have many temple statues like other nations
He changes the subject to the date of Passover
questions.answer: He affirms Christians do not use images in worship and gives a distinct rationale from other image-less groups

questions.question: What is Ortlund’s point about Tertullian’s references to paintings on communion cups in On Modesty?
options: Tertullian is instituting mandatory iconostases
Didactic/figurative use on vessels falls short of Nicaea II’s cultic veneration of holy icons
Tertullian approves Dionysian bacchic cups for the Eucharist
Tertullian denies the second commandment entirely in that chapter
questions.answer: Didactic/figurative use on vessels falls short of Nicaea II’s cultic veneration of holy icons

questions.question: How does Ortlund treat Methodius’s reference to images of angels?
options: As definitive proof of seventh-century icon theology in the second century
As identical to the Lycurgus Cup’s Dionysiac cult
Making images of angels is not the same as cultic veneration as defined at Nicaea II
As requiring prostration before every angel mosaic
questions.answer: Making images of angels is not the same as cultic veneration as defined at Nicaea II

questions.question: Mike Humphrey (as summarized) associates the decisive shift of images into “icons” through which the saint is manifested with roughly which period?
options: The apostolic age in the 30s AD
The Council of Trent exclusively
The Protestant Reformation only
Circa 680 AD (with scholarly debate vs a somewhat earlier late-sixth-century timeline)
questions.answer: Circa 680 AD (with scholarly debate vs a somewhat earlier late-sixth-century timeline)

questions.question: Which western synod does Ortlund positively contrast with Nicaea II’s theology on images?
options: Council of Frankfurt opposing image adoration
Council of Orange on grace
Fourth Lateran on transubstantiation
Vatican I on papal infallibility
questions.answer: Council of Frankfurt opposing image adoration

questions.question: Ortlund quotes Luther at Worms as denying ultimate authority to what?
options: The Old Testament alone
The pope and councils alone, since they have erred and contradicted each other
Baptism and the Lord’s Supper
The German princes collectively
questions.answer: The pope and councils alone, since they have erred and contradicted each other

questions.question: Eusebius’s letter to Constantia is cited to show what attitude toward images of Christ?
options: Encouraging her to commission icons for every chapel
Approving only if the icons are kissed daily
Rebuking the request using the second commandment and stressing such things are excluded from churches worldwide
Silence—he never addressed the topic
questions.answer: Rebuking the request using the second commandment and stressing such things are excluded from churches worldwide

questions.question: Ortlund says Gregory the Great still conceives of images primarily how?
options: As objects for bowing and prayer through figural mediation
As identical to pagan idols in every respect
As mandatory replacements for Scripture reading
As purely didactic/teaching aids, not tied to the Nicaea II-style cultic use
questions.answer: As purely didactic/teaching aids, not tied to the Nicaea II-style cultic use

questions.question: Why does Ortlund argue Nicaea I is “a completely different ball game” from Nicaea II regarding biblical grounding?
options: Because the New Testament themes of Christ’s deity and worship are pervasive in a way he thinks unlike grounding Nicaea II in Scripture
Because Nicaea I avoided all scriptural arguments
Because Nicaea I was not ecumenical
Because Nicaea I only discussed icon colors
questions.answer: Because the New Testament themes of Christ’s deity and worship are pervasive in a way he thinks unlike grounding Nicaea II in Scripture

questions.question: When does Ortlund say he released his earlier major video arguing icon veneration is an accretion?
options: May 2024
January 2023
December 325
October 1517
questions.answer: January 2023
